claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 8 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you applying Child Benefit for, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my three children. Their names are Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're enquiring about Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Before I assess your eligibility, let me first check the official rules to ensure I'm applying the correct criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. Now I need to gather some information about your specific circumstances. Let me ask you one question at a time:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship to each child.

**Do you live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie, or do any of them live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Blake live with me at home, but Charlie doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Charlie doesn't live with you, I need to understand more about that arrangement.

**Are you making any financial contributions towards Charlie's upkeep (such as money for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to check the ages of your children to determine eligibility.

**How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 10, Blake is 19, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to clarify Blake's education and training status since they're 19.

**Is Blake in approved education or training (such as school, college, university, or an apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Now I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each child.

Let me review the rules and make my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on the information you've provided, here's your eligibility status:

**✓ ELIGIBLE - Alex (age 10)**
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex, as they live with you in the UK and meet all the eligibility requirements.

**✗ NOT ELIGIBLE - Blake (age 19)**
You cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. Although Blake lives with you, they are 19 years old and no longer in approved education or training. Child Benefit for those aged 16 and over only continues if they remain in full-time approved education or training.

**✗ NOT ELIGIBLE - Charlie (age 2)**
You cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie as they don't live with you and you're not making any financial contributions towards their upkeep. The rules allow for 8 weeks of payments after a child moves away, but after that period, you can only continue receiving payments if you contribute financially to their upkeep by at least the amount of the Child Benefit payment itself.

You should apply for Child Benefit for Alex. If your circumstances change—for example, if you start contributing financially to Charlie's upkeep, or if Blake enters approved education or training—you may become eligible for them in the future.

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as you live with them. Alex meets all the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16, living with you, and you live in the UK.

Blake is 19 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the rules, you normally only qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over if they remain in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old but does not live with you, and you are not making any financial contributions towards Charlie's upkeep. According to the rules, you would normally get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims. However, after this 8-week period, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if you contribute to the child's upkeep by at least the amount of the Child Benefit payment. Since you are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie (assuming the 8-week period has passed or you wish to claim beyond it).
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria as a 10-year-old child living with you in the UK. Blake, at 19 and not in approved education or training, is no longer eligible. Charlie, at 2 years old but not living with you and with no financial contributions being made towards their upkeep, is also not eligible for Child Benefit beyond any initial 8-week period after they moved away.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as you live with them. Alex meets all the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: under 16, living with you, and you live in the UK.

Blake is 19 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the rules, you normally only qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over if they remain in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old but does not live with you, and you are not making any financial contributions towards Charlie's upkeep. According to the rules, you would normally get Child Benefit for 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else if nobody else claims. However, after this 8-week period, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if you contribute to the child's upkeep by at least the amount of the Child Benefit payment. Since you are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie (assuming the 8-week period has passed or you wish to claim beyond it).

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria as a 10-year-old child living with you in the UK. Blake, at 19 and not in approved education or training, is no longer eligible. Charlie, at 2 years old but not living with you and with no financial contributions being made towards their upkeep, is also not eligible for Child Benefit beyond any initial 8-week period after they moved away.
